data.title: CME Data: Low Probability of Fed Rate Hike in April
data.description: According to Jin10, CME's FedWatch tool indicates a 7.2% probability of a 25 basis point rate hike by the Federal Reserve in April. The likelihood of maintaining the current interest rate stands at 92.8%. Looking ahead to June, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point increase is 9.2%, while the chance of a 50 basis point hike is just 0.2%. The probability of rates remaining unchanged by June is 90.6%.

data.title: Stablecoins Seen as Core Infrastructure by Banks, Says Circle
data.description: Circle has announced that banks are increasingly considering stablecoins as a fundamental part of their infrastructure strategy. According to NS3.AI, this shift comes as 24-hour payment systems begin to integrate with traditional financial systems. Circle emphasized that regulatory frameworks are playing a crucial role in driving the adoption of stablecoins, transitioning tokenized money from pilot projects into operational planning.

data.title: UAE Dismantles Terror Network Linked to Hezbollah and Iran
data.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has announced the dismantling of a terror network allegedly funded and directed by Hezbollah and Iran. According to Jin10, the UAE authorities have taken action against the group, which was reportedly involved in activities threatening regional stability. The network's operations were said to be orchestrated with support from Hezbollah, a Lebanese militant group, and Iran, raising concerns about the influence of these entities in the region. The UAE's move underscores its commitment to combating terrorism and maintaining security within its borders. Further details about the operation and the individuals involved have not been disclosed.

data.title: U.S. Senators Propose Mandatory Fertilizer Price Reporting Bill
data.description: A bipartisan group of U.S. senators has introduced legislation aimed at enforcing mandatory price reporting for fertilizers. Bloomberg posted on X that the proposed bill seeks to enhance transparency in the agricultural sector by requiring detailed price disclosures from fertilizer producers. The senators argue that this measure will help farmers make informed decisions and potentially reduce costs. The bill reflects ongoing concerns about the volatility of fertilizer prices and their impact on farming operations. The initiative is part of broader efforts to address agricultural challenges and support the farming community.

data.title: Cuban Ambassador Rejects U.S. Pressure to Remove President Diaz-Canel
data.description: Cuba's ambassador to the United Nations has firmly stated that removing President Miguel Diaz-Canel to satisfy U.S. demands is not an option. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing that any discussions between the two nations should be grounded in 'mutual respect.' The ambassador's comments highlight the ongoing tensions between Cuba and the United States, as both countries navigate their diplomatic relationship.

data.title: Global central banks are on high alert to combat a resurgence of inflation, putting downward pressure on the dollar, which fell by more than 1%.
data.description: Non-US currencies strengthened across the board, putting pressure on the US dollar index, which fell more than 1% in late New York trading on Thursday. This week saw a rare "super central bank week," with central banks in the US, Japan, the UK, Canada, and the Eurozone holding policy meetings, as well as several emerging economies. On Wednesday, the Federal Reserve and the Bank of Canada both decided to keep interest rates unchanged; on Thursday, the Bank of Japan, the Bank of England, the European Central Bank, and the central banks of Switzerland and Sweden made the same decision. These central banks made it clear they would remain vigilant, concerned that rising energy prices could trigger a wave of inflation across the wider economy. Even the Brazilian central bank, which has the highest interest rate among major economies, opted to cut its benchmark rate only slightly by 25 basis points to 14.75%, while the market had expected a 50 basis point cut. "This escalation in Iran seems to be a turning point for the market, as the conflict is no longer just about military headlines or the Strait of Hormuz blockade," said Haru Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo Bank in Singapore. "It's now impacting the lifeline of the global energy system. What's currently unsettling the market is the growing risk of stagflation."

data.title: John Chirico Retires from Citi After Decades of Service
data.description: John Chirico, a prominent figure in the banking industry, is retiring from his role as global investment banking chair at Citi. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Chirico's extensive career, during which he managed major clients such as Verizon, Tesla, and SpaceX. His departure marks the end of a significant era for Citi, where he played a crucial role in handling top-tier clients and contributing to the bank's global investment banking operations. Chirico's retirement is seen as a notable transition for the institution, reflecting his impactful tenure and the relationships he fostered with key corporate clients over the years.

data.title: UK Ministers May Delay Shipbuilding Projects Amid Defense Budget Cuts
data.description: UK ministers are considering delaying shipbuilding projects in response to a proposed £10 billion reduction in the defense budget, according to Jin10. The potential budget cuts have prompted discussions within the government about prioritizing spending and possibly postponing certain naval construction initiatives. The move comes as the UK seeks to balance its defense commitments with fiscal constraints, raising concerns about the impact on the country's naval capabilities and defense industry. The decision is part of broader efforts to manage public finances while maintaining national security.

data.title: Tricolor Founder Faces Trial Over Alleged Fraud Charges
data.description: Daniel Chu, the founder of Tricolor, is scheduled to stand trial in October on allegations of defrauding banks and investors linked to the used-car dealership. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the charges that emerged following the company's collapse last year. The trial will examine the accusations against Chu, which have drawn significant attention due to the impact on financial institutions and stakeholders involved with Tricolor. The case underscores the legal challenges faced by business leaders in the automotive sector.

data.title: Canada's Conservative Leader Advocates Free Market Policies on Podcast
data.description: Canada's Conservative Party Leader Pierre Poilievre recently appeared on The Joe Rogan Experience podcast, where he discussed his stance against tariffs and promoted free-market principles. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Poilievre's emphasis on economic freedom and his critique of government intervention in markets. Poilievre argued that reducing tariffs would benefit consumers and businesses by lowering costs and increasing competition. He also stressed the importance of individual liberty and the role of government in facilitating a thriving economy. Poilievre's appearance on the popular podcast is part of his broader strategy to reach a wider audience and communicate his economic vision.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Decline in Metals Prices Impacts S&P/TSX Composite Index
data.description: The S&P/TSX Composite Index has experienced a decline, largely due to significant drops in metals prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the index's vulnerability given its substantial exposure to the metals sector. This downturn reflects broader market trends affecting commodities and related industries.

data.title: EU Leaders Address Migration Surge Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The European Union (EU) leaders have issued a statement highlighting the impact of the ongoing conflict in Iran, which has led to a surge in migration towards the EU. According to Jin10, the EU is prepared to utilize its full range of diplomatic, legal, operational, and financial tools to prevent an uncontrolled influx of migrants and to safeguard European security. The statement underscores the EU's commitment to managing migration effectively while addressing the root causes of displacement. The leaders emphasized the importance of maintaining stability and security within the region, as well as collaborating with international partners to address the challenges posed by the migration crisis.

data.title: Information Technology Sector Leads in Buy Ratings
data.description: The Information Technology sector within the $SPX index currently holds the highest percentage of Buy ratings among all 11 sectors, standing at 68%. FactSet posted on X, highlighting this trend in the market. This indicates a strong investor confidence in the sector's potential for growth and profitability. The data reflects a positive outlook for technology companies, which continue to attract significant interest from investors.

data.title: Digital Asset Firms Shift to Debt and Bitcoin-Backed Loans Amid Equity Premium Decline
data.description: Digital-asset treasury firms are increasingly turning to debt, preferred shares, and Bitcoin-backed loans as equity premiums decrease. According to NS3.AI, Strategy's STRC is offering an 11.5% annual yield, which is reset monthly. This approach aims to mitigate common-share dilution but introduces additional leverage and duration risk to corporate structures already linked to Bitcoin.

data.title: Microsoft's MAI-Image-2 Model Achieves High Ranking on Arena.ai Leaderboard
data.description: Microsoft has introduced its MAI-Image-2 text-to-image model, which has secured the third position on the Arena.ai leaderboard. According to NS3.AI, the model is currently accessible in the MAI Playground, with plans for a phased integration into Copilot and Bing Image Creator. The model's current functionality is limited by strict content filters, a 30-second cooldown period, a daily cap of 15 images, and a restriction to 1:1 output.

data.title: EU Leaders Condemn Iran's Indiscriminate Military Strikes in Region
data.description: The European Council has issued a strong condemnation of Iran's indiscriminate military strikes against countries in the region. According to Jin10, the statement was made during a summit of EU leaders, where they expressed their support for the affected nations. The council emphasized the importance of regional stability and called for an end to hostilities. The EU leaders reiterated their commitment to working with international partners to address the ongoing tensions and promote peace in the region.

data.title: EU Leaders Urge Restraint and Protection of Civilians Amid Tensions
data.description: The European Council has issued a statement calling for all parties involved in ongoing tensions to exercise maximum restraint and protect civilians and civilian infrastructure. According to Jin10, the council emphasized the importance of fully adhering to international law and urged a halt to strikes on energy and water facilities. The statement reflects the EU's commitment to promoting peace and stability in the region.

data.title: City's Resilience in Overcoming Crises Explored
data.description: A recent documentary examines the city's historical resilience in facing crises and its potential to recover from current challenges. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the city's ability to navigate through difficult times and the strategies that may aid its recovery. The documentary delves into past experiences and lessons learned, offering insights into how the city can emerge stronger from its present situation.

data.title: Global LNG Buyers Turn to U.S. Amid Qatar Facility Attack
data.description: On March 20, Jin10 reported that following an attack on a major liquefied natural gas (LNG) facility in Qatar, global fuel supply tensions have intensified. According to foreign media, this has led to an increasing number of buyers and importers urgently seeking LNG supplies from the United States. According to informed sources, companies aiming to import LNG have directly contacted U.S. fuel sellers. These sellers will provide supplies for both existing and under-construction projects. The United States, currently the world's largest LNG exporter, plans to further expand its export capacity through several planned or under-construction facilities.

data.title: French Aircraft Carrier's Location Revealed by Fitness App
data.description: A French naval officer inadvertently disclosed the location of the aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle by uploading his running records to the fitness platform Strava. According to Jin10, the data indicated that the carrier is currently positioned northwest of Cyprus, approximately 100 kilometers from the Turkish coast. This incident highlights potential security risks associated with the use of fitness tracking applications by military personnel.
